title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: Department of Energy’s tech incubator doubles down on fusion power
items.description: ARPA-E’s $135 million in new funds will match its total spending from previous 12 years

items.title: NSF names record number of graduate fellows, rebounding from 2025 dip
items.description: Distribution by field is also closer to the norm than last year’s class

items.title: Penguins become marine detectives, thanks to pollutant-detecting anklets
items.description: Scientific jewelry picks up “forever chemicals” that can harm wildlife and humans

items.title: Trump seeks to jump-start long-planned Antarctic research icebreaker
items.description: White House’s 2027 budget request includes $900 million for NSF to upgrade U.S. academic fleet

items.title: Differences in cells’ protein factories may help explain human diversity
items.description: Study links mutations in ribosome genes to traits such as height and weight

items.title: Why do anglerfish have glowing lures? It might be sex
items.description: Species proliferated after evolving bioluminescence, possibly because mates could find each other more easily

items.title: A galaxy seems to host two giant black holes, poised to collide in a century
items.description: Unusual radio signals could be long-sought smoking gun of galactic mergers

items.title: Roaming gangs of tumor cells help spread cancer. Can drugs break them up?
items.description: To impede metastasis, researchers seek to develop novel treatments that disrupt tumor cell clusters

items.title: What plunged these chimps into civil war? A new study traces the breakdown
items.description: Decades of observations tracked the fraying of once-friendly relations among Ugandan chimpanzees

items.title: Did quantum sensors help find a U.S. pilot shot down in Iran? Experts doubt it
items.description: The heart’s faint magnetic signals can’t be detected at large distances, researchers say
